name: AI Infrastructure Optimizer
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for infrastructure analysis, optimization reasoning, and natural language cost reports

services.name: Azure Monitor
services.purpose: Resource metrics collection — CPU, memory, network, disk utilization across subscriptions

services.name: Azure Advisor
services.purpose: Built-in optimization recommendations — right-sizing, reserved instances, idle resources

services.name: Cost Management
services.purpose: Cost data export, budget tracking, anomaly detection, forecast APIs

prod.note: Scheduled exports to storage, API access

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Optimizer API — analysis orchestrator, recommendation engine, report generator

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Optimization history, recommendation tracking, baseline snapshots, trend data

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: API keys, subscription credentials, management API secrets

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Optimizer performance, recommendation accuracy, savings tracking dashboards

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for routine cost summaries, reserve GPT-4o for complex multi-resource optimization analysis — saves 65% on AI costs
Schedule deep optimization scans during off-peak hours — reduces compute contention and costs
Cache Azure Advisor recommendations with 1-hour TTL — API calls are free but latency improves with caching
Use Cost Management scheduled exports instead of real-time API calls — reduces API overhead
The optimizer should identify savings that exceed its own cost — target 10x ROI on optimizer spend
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
